questions.questionText:
A flight departs Singapore at $21\text{:}50$ on Tuesday (UTC+8)
and arrives in London at $05\text{:}25$ on Wednesday (UTC+0).
Calculate the flight duration in hours and minutes.

markScheme.desc: Convert departure to UTC: $21\text{:}50$ UTC+8 $= 13\text{:}50$ UTC (Tuesday)

questions.explanation: Convert all times to the same time zone before calculating duration.

questions.questionText:
A train leaves at $08\text{:}47$ and the journey should take $3$ hours $28$ minutes.
Due to a $15$-minute delay, calculate the actual arrival time.
questions.explanation: Add hours and minutes separately; regroup minutes to hours if needed.

questions.context:
The table shows train departure times from City A to City B:

| Train | Departs A | Arrives B |
|-------|-----------|-----------|
| P | 06:15 | 08:42 |
| Q | 09:30 | 11:48 |
| R | 13:55 | 16:10 |

parts.label: a
parts.questionText: Which train has the shortest journey time?

markScheme.desc: Calculates all three durations: P = 2h 27min, Q = 2h 18min, R = 2h 15min

parts.label: b
parts.questionText:
Carlo arrives at City A at $09\text{:}45$.
He catches the next available train.
At what time does he arrive in City B?

markScheme.desc: Train Q departs $09\text{:}30$ — already departed; next train is R at $13\text{:}55$

workingSteps: Carlo arrives at $09\text{:}45$; Train Q left at $09\text{:}30$ — missed
Next train: Train R departs $13\text{:}55$, arrives $16\text{:}10$
